audit_status.source_base: Greek Ephesians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for imitation/love, light, wisdom, Spirit-filled life, mutual submission, marriage household code with domination safeguard.

verses.restored: Become therefore imitators of the Divine, as beloved children,
and walk in love, just as also Messiah loved us and gave himself up for us, an offering and sacrifice to the Divine for fragrant aroma.
But sexual immorality/misuse and every uncleanness or greed, let it not even be named among you, as is fitting for holy ones,
and shamefulness and foolish talk or coarse joking, which are not fitting, but rather thanksgiving.
For this you know, recognizing that every sexually immoral/misusing or unclean or greedy person, who is an idolater, has no inheritance in the reign of Messiah and Divine.
Let no one deceive you with empty words, for because of these things the wrath of the Divine comes upon the children of disobedience.
Therefore do not become co-participants with them.
For you were once darkness, but now light in the Lord; walk as children of light,
for the fruit of light is in all goodness and righteousness/justice and truth,
testing what is pleasing to the Lord.
And do not participate in the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather expose them.
For the things done by them in secret are shameful even to speak of.
But all things being exposed by the light are made manifest,
for everything being made manifest is light. Therefore it says: Wake, sleeper, and rise from the dead, and Messiah will shine upon you.
Therefore watch carefully how you walk, not as unwise but as wise,
redeeming the season, because the days are harmful.
Because of this, do not become foolish, but understand what is the will of the Lord.
And do not be drunk with wine, in which is excess, but be filled in Spirit,
speaking to one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord,
giving thanks always for all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Messiah to the Divine and Father,
being subject to one another in reverence/fear of Messiah.
Wives, to your own husbands as to the Lord,
because a husband is head/source of the wife as also Messiah is head/source of the assembly, he himself savior/healer of the body.
But as the assembly is subject to Messiah, so also wives to husbands in everything.
Husbands, love the wives, just as Messiah also loved the assembly and gave himself up for her,
so that he might sanctify her, cleansing by the washing of water in word,
so that he might present to himself the assembly in glory, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she may be holy and blameless.
So also husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. The one loving his own wife loves himself.
For no one ever hated one's own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as also Messiah the assembly,
because we are members of his body.
For this reason a human will leave father and mother and be joined to wife, and the two will be into one flesh.
This mystery is great; but I speak into Messiah and into the assembly.
Nevertheless, you also, each one, let each love his own wife thus as himself, and the wife so that she may reverence the husband.

safety_note: Ephesians 5 household language must not be used to sanctify domination or abuse. The controlling frame is verse 21, mutual subjection in reverence of Messiah, and the husband's command is self-giving love patterned on Messiah, not control. Head/source language is debated and should be handled carefully.
